description_long: This house features a two-story structure with a steeply pitched, reddish-brown gable roof and a prominent stone chimney. The exterior walls on the lower level are constructed from light-colored stone, while the upper level is clad in smooth, light gray panels framed by exposed wooden beams. The front facade displays a row of rectangular windows set between wooden posts, and a wooden door is visible at the base, partially open. An additional roofed section extends from one side, likely serving as a small annex or storage area. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional European or Tudor-style homes, with a blend of stone and timber elements.
description: A two-story house with a steep gable roof, stone lower walls, timber-framed upper level, and a prominent chimney. Features include rectangular windows, a wooden door, and an annex with a sloped roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, a row of windows, and the annex with a sloped roof on the left.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the stone chimney and a single window, with the gable roof clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with symmetrical window arrangement and the annex roof extending from the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the annex roof in the foreground and a single window on the upper level.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door at ground level, no porch, with stone steps and minimal decorative elements.
